prose:
Calculation summary (method–provenance block)

Property: Frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap; units: eV  
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted RI-JK)  
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz; single-point only (no re-optimization) → notation: DFT//given-xyz  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent)  
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)  
Software/backend: PySCF (version not reported by engine) via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill  
SCF convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 9 cycles; no warnings reported by engine.  
Live log: a real-time stdout/stderr transcript was written during the run (frontier_orbitals_… .out) alongside the JSON result; see your working directory for inspection.

Computed results

• HOMO energy = –6.3597638 eV  
• LUMO energy = –0.1024036 eV  
• HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV

All numbers are electronic orbital eigenvalues (Koopmans energies) at the stated level of theory; the gap is not an optical excitation energy.

The computation used chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill with explicit flags  
`--method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1`, calling PySCF under the hood.

Engine integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings reported.
